revision.comment: Bot: mark 3 deleted QID(s) as DELETED_QID in ill templates [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23989927232|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:針博士]]
'''Hari Hakase''' (針博士) was a position under the {{ill|Ministry of the Imperial Household (Ritsuryō system)|ja|宮内省 (律令制)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ministry of the Imperial Household (Ritsuryō system)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ministry of the Imperial Household (Ritsuryō system)|1=Ministry of the Imperial Household (Ritsuryō system)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q125558338}} in the {{ill|Bureau of Medicine|ja|典薬寮|1=Bureau of Pharmacy|2=ja|3=典薬寮|4=fr|5=Bureau de pharmacie|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bureau of Medicine|13=User:Immanuelle/Bureau of Pharmacy|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bureau of Medicine|13=User:Immanuelle/Bureau of Pharmacy|qq=}}. The role corresponded to the Rank of {{ill|Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従七位下|1=Junior Seventh Rank|lt=Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade|3=従七位|1=Junior Seventh Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Seventh Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Seventh Rank|ja_comment=former now fixed redirect|3=従七位|ja_comment=former now fixed redirect|3=従七位}} and had a single appointment. However, many individuals in this position were promoted to Ranks higher than Junior Seventh Rank. The Hari Hakase was responsible for the education of {{ill|Harisei|ja|針生|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harisei|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harisei|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and the supervision of {{ill|Harishi|ja|針師|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harishi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harishi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (needle practitioners). The position was also referred to as "Shin Hakase" (鍼博士). == Overview == The {{ill|Taihō Code|en|Taihō Code|qid=Q1059892|lt=Taihō Code|1=Taihō Code}}'s {{ill|Medical Ordinances|ja|医疾令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Medical Ordinances|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Medical Ordinances|1=Medical Ordinances|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11404630}}, promulgated in {{ill|701|en|701|qid=Q23496|lt=701|1=701}} (Taihō 1), and the {{ill|Yōrō Code|en|Yōrō Code|qid=Q2575743|lt=Yōrō Code|1=Yōrō Code}}'s Medical Ordinances, issued in {{ill|AD 757|en|AD 757|qid=Q31135|lt=757|1=AD 757}} (Tenpyō Hōji 1), established the Hari Hakase position. The Hari Hakase oversaw the training of 30 Harisei, who underwent two years of general education and seven years of specialized training (including practical skills and reading specialized texts). After passing a graduation and appointment examination conducted by the Hari Hakase, successful candidates became Harishi and practiced {{ill|acupuncture|ja|針治療|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/acupuncture|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/acupuncture|ja_comment=former now fixed redirect|3=鍼|ja_comment=former now fixed redirect|3=鍼|1=Acupuncture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q121713}}. The most capable among them could later be appointed as Hari Hakase. Notably, {{ill|Tanba Yasuyori|ja|丹波康頼|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanba Yasuyori|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanba Yasuyori|1=Tanba no Yasuyori|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q1466794}}, who authored the {{ill|Ishinpō|en|Ishinpō|qid=Q649560|lt=Ishinpō|1=Ishinpō}}, held this position. The Ishinpō is regarded as Japan's oldest medical compendium.<ref>[http://www.tau.ac.jp/future/acupuncture/history.html 鍼灸の歴史] Tokyo Ariake University of Medical and Health Sciences</ref> In the {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|qid=Q193292|lt=Heian period|1=Heian period}}, the Hari Hakase was initially appointed based on ability and was akin to a modern professorship. However, over time, it became a ceremonial title. During the {{ill|Muromachi period|en|Muromachi period|qid=Q334845|lt=Muromachi period|1=Muromachi period}}, the medical official system was abolished, and the Hari Hakase position declined in importance.<ref>{{Cite journal |和書|author=Yoshida Kazuyasu |title=History of Acupuncture in Japan from the Muromachi to Edo Periods |journal=Journal of Social Acupuncture Studies |year=2011 |issue=5 |pages=32-53 |url=https://hdl.handle.net/10460/1741 |publisher=Society of Social Acupuncture Studies|qq=}}</ref> Some accounts suggest the Hari Hakase position continued until the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|en|Meiji Restoration|qid=Q8707|lt=Meiji Restoration|1=Meiji Restoration}}.<ref>{{Cite journal |和書|author=Kawamata Jun'ichi |title=Eastern and Western Medicine |journal=Cancer and Humanity |publisher=Osaka Cancer Research Society |year=1994 |issue=21 |pages=4-6 |naid=120005248780 |url=https://hdl.handle.net/11094/23958|qq=}}</ref> == Historical Hari Hakase == * {{ill|Sugawara Kadenari|ja|菅原梶成|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara Kadenari|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara Kadenari|1=Sugawara no Kajinari|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11619194}} - Traveled to {{ill|Tang dynasty|en|Tang dynasty|qid=Q9683|lt=Tang China|1=Tang dynasty}} during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Wenzong of Tang|ja|唐の文宗|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Wenzong of Tang|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Wenzong of Tang|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Upon returning to Japan, he served as Hari Hakase and attained the Rank of Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade, and became a {{ill|Imperial Physician|ja|侍医|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Physician|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Physician|ja_comment=former now fixed redirect|3=内薬司|ja_comment=former now fixed redirect|3=内薬司|1=court physician|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11394428}}.<ref name="shimizu_ikakikisiwa1">Shimizu Kyōzō, "History of Japanese Medical Instruments (1)," Journal of the Japanese Society of Medical Instruments, Vol. 39, No. 2, February 1, 1969, pp. 133-137.</ref> * {{ill|Fukane Munetsugu|ja|深根宗継|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukane Munetsugu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukane Munetsugu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=DELETED_QID|1=Q135500668}}<ref>『{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|en|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|qid=Q1052981|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}』, Volume 12, Entry for January 7, Jōgan 8 (866).</ref> * {{ill|Shimomichi Kadosugu|ja|下道門継|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimomichi Kadosugu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimomichi Kadosugu|1=Shimotsumichi no Kadotsugu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q45828681}}<ref>『日本三代実録』, Volume 26, Entry for August 9, Jōgan 16 (874).</ref> * {{ill|Katsuragi Takamune|ja|葛城高宗|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi Takamune|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi Takamune|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=DELETED_QID|1=Q135500672}}<ref>『日本三代実録』, Volume 30, Entry for January 3, Gangyō 1 (877).</ref> * {{ill|Ahiko Ujio|ja|阿比古氏雄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ahiko Ujio|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ahiko Ujio|1=Ahiko Ujio|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q96273557}} - Mentioned as Imperial Physician and Hari Hakase, Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade, in the entry for January 7, Ninna 2 (886).<ref>『日本三代実録』, Volume 49, Entry for January 7, Ninna 2 (886).</ref> * {{ill|Tanba Yasuyori|ja|丹波康頼|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanba Yasuyori|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanba Yasuyori|1=Tanba no Yasuyori|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q1466794}} - Author of Ishinpō. His descendants included many Hari Hakase and heads of the Bureau of Medicine. Notable descendants include {{ill|Taki Yasutaka|ja|多紀安琢|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taki Yasutaka|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taki Yasutaka|1=Taki Antaku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11431003}}, a director of the Shogunate Medical Academy, and actor [[:en:Tetsurō Tanba|Tanba Tetsurō]]. * {{ill|Tanba Tadayaki|ja|丹波忠明|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanba Tadayaki|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanba Tadayaki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=DELETED_QID|1=Q135500675}} - Grandson of Tanba Yasuyori. Celebrated as a master acupuncturist, he served as Deputy Hari Hakase and later as head of the Bureau of Medicine, achieving the Fourth Rank. His son, {{ill|Tanba Masatada|ja|丹波雅忠|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanba Masatada|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanba Masatada|1=Tanba no Masatada|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q17212991}}, was renowned as "Japan's {{ill|Bian Que|en|Bian Que|qid=Q855326|lt=Bian Que|1=Bian Que}}," and both gained widespread fame. == References == {{Reflist|qq=}} == Further Reading == * {{Citation|和書|author=Keizai Zasshisha |title=National Historical Series, Volume 4: Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|publisher=Keizai Zasshisha|date=1901|url={{NDLDC|991094|qq=}}|qq=}}
{{translated page|ja|針博士|version=100281804|comment=Imported full ja history}}
[[ja:針博士]]
